item.title: News Publishers Weigh Whether AI is Industry Killer or Savior
item.description: The potential effects of AI dominate a meeting of global publishers

item.title: Bessent Plays Down a $200 Gasoline-Bill Hike Hurting US Buying Power
item.description: The added US gas burden is one part of an increase in the cost of living that’s outpacing wages.

item.title: EU Warns Bulgaria of Excessive Deficit Months After Joining Euro
item.description: The European Union recommended adding Bulgaria to a list of countries violating budget balance rules just six months after the Balkan country adopted the euro.

item.title: EU Allows Room for Energy Crisis Aid, But With a Green Emphasis
item.description: The European Union will give extra budget leeway to member states to cope with high energy prices stoked by the Iran war, so long as it helps curb consumption of fossil fuels.

item.title: Global Businesses Worry About Fresh Dose of US Tariff Complexity
item.description: The proposed duties will go through a public comment period and bilateral talks before implementation

item.title: JPMorgan’s Lipikhina Sees Earnings Supercycle Driving US Stocks
item.description: An earnings “supercycle” in the US is poised to drive stocks to more record highs, according to Nataliia Lipikhina at JPMorgan Chase & Co., fueled by heavy spending from hyperscalers and advances in agentic AI.

item.title: Korea, Taiwan Stock Rallies Fail to Wow Foreign Investors
item.description: Cash is flowing into Japanese equities.

item.title: Hedge Fund Targets Ferrari Owners in Private Credit Exotics Boom
item.description: Private credit’s getting into the luxury car business.

item.title: SpaceX Kicks Off AI IPOs Set to Rewrite the Rules of Wall Street
item.description: The AI era is set to enrich Wall Street in a way different from previous technology booms.

item.title: Putin’s Next Ombudsman Wants Less Jail Time for Businessmen
item.description: Russia’s next business ombudsman wants to see fewer entrepreneurs winding up in jail or pre-trial detention, arguing that imprisonment remains overused in cases of economic crimes despite years of legal reforms.

item.title: Ghana’s Inflation Rate Edges Up as Iran Conflict Stokes Prices
item.description: Ghana’s annual inflation rate edged higher in May as the Iran war fanned price pressures, increasing the likelihood that the central bank will keep interest rates on hold when it next meets.

item.title: Spain Earmarks Up to €800M for Massive EU Data Center Project
item.description: Spain is confident it’ll be awarded a massive artificial intelligence data center project by the EU and plans to invest as much as €800 million ($929 million) in it.

item.title: Bahrain Taps Dollar Bond Market Hours After Missile Attack
item.description: Bahrain is marketing a dollar bond hours after fending off Iranian missile attacks, in a key test of investor appetite for Middle East debt.

item.title: AI Music Startup Suno Raises Capital at $5.4 Billion Valuation
item.description: Suno Inc., a startup that enables people to make music using artificial intelligence, has raised $400 million at a valuation of $5.4 billion.

item.title: DeepSeek Close to Sealing $7 Billion Funding in Historic AI Deal
item.description: DeepSeek is close to finalizing a deal to raise about 50 billion yuan ($7.4 billion) from investors including Tencent Holdings Ltd. and its own founder in one of China’s largest startup financings, a milestone for a domestic AI industry seeking capital to take on marquee US names like OpenAI.

item.title: Uber Targets Doubling Its Fleet of Electric Motorcycles in Kenya
item.description: Uber Technologies Inc plans to double its fleet of electric motorcycles in Kenya by the end of the year, as the company races toward becoming a zero-emissions mobility platform globally by 2040.

item.title: Nestle Buys Out Yfood Founders in First Acquisition for New CEO
item.description: Nestle SA is taking full control of ready-to-drink meal maker yfood Labs GmbH, bolstering the company’s push to focus on faster-growing brands, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Treasuries Eye Biggest Drop In Two Weeks on Fed Rate-Hike Bets
item.description: Treasuries slid, heading for their biggest drop in more than two weeks, after an escalation in US-Iran tensions lifted oil prices and added to expectations that the Federal Reserve may raise interest rates this year.

item.title: Broadcom’s $280 Billion, Four-Day Bonanza to Get Earnings Check
item.description: Broadcom Inc. shares have been on a tear, adding more than $280 billion in market value in just the last four sessions. But with earnings arriving after the bell, investors will have a chance to assess whether the rally has gone too far.

item.title: Deutsche Bank to Set Aside More Than Expected for Souring Credit
item.description: Deutsche Bank AG may set aside more money than analysts had expected for souring loans in the three months through June as it cleans up its balance sheet.

item.title: Ghana Raises Growth Forecast as Reform Gains Weather Iran Impact
item.description: Ghana’s economy is growing faster than expected as companies benefit from lower inflation and interest rates, with budget and foreign-exchange reforms helping offset the impact from the war in Iran, Finance Minister Cassiel Ato Forson said.

item.title: BNP Fund Beating 97% of Peers Pivots to SMR Stocks After Selloff
item.description: A fund run by BNP Paribas Asset Management that’s outperformed 97% of its peers over the past year says it’s now moving into small-modular-reactor stocks after long shunning the sector as a “non-viable” investment.

item.title: PGIM Bets on Hungary Extending Best Emerging-Market Bond Rally
item.description: Hungary’s bonds, the world’s best emerging-market performers in the past months, are set to rally further, according to asset manager PGIM Ltd.

item.title: S. Korea’s Ruling Party Set for Big Election Win, Poll Shows
item.description: South Korea’s ruling Democratic Party is on track to win a clear majority of key local offices in a nationwide vote, providing President Lee Jae Myung a boost as he enters his second year in office.

item.title: Nvidia CEO Pitches ‘Insane’ AI Returns to Billionaire Families
item.description: Nvidia Corp. Chief Executive Officer Jensen Huang touted “insane” returns for investors willing to bet on the AI boom, seeking to dispel lingering concerns around the big sums spent on artificial intelligence and its long-term profitability.

item.title: HSBC Asset’s Little Sees Weaker Dollar on Shift in Data Reaction
item.description: HSBC Asset Management expects the dollar to weaken, arguing that its limited gains despite favorable macro conditions may point to a structural downtrend once energy shocks and geopolitical tensions fade.

item.title: EU Risks Losing More Than 1 Million Jobs Over Global Pressures
item.description: The European Union is at risk of losing more than 1 million jobs this year over the Iran war’s economic fallout and rising global competition, the bloc’s executive warned.

item.title: India Said to Ready Tax Cut Plans to Attract Foreign Bond Buyers
item.description: India is poised to announce steps to draw more foreign investments by reducing taxes and removing caps on the ownership of some bonds as soon as this week, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: Ueda Says BOJ Needs to Keep Raising Rates to Contain Inflation
item.description: The Bank of Japan needs to keep raising interest rates in response to developments in the economy and inflation, Governor Kazuo Ueda said in his final scheduled speaking event ahead of a closely watched policy meeting.

item.title: Why the Strait of Hormuz Is at the Heart of the US-Iran Stalemate
item.description: No region of the world produces more oil and gas than the countries straddling the Persian Gulf. Most of this energy can only be exported aboard tankers that cross the Strait of Hormuz — a waterway that’s effectively been blocked for more than three months.
